2605.19855.title: A Framework for Evaluating Zero-Shot Image Generation in Concept-based Explainability
2605.19855.paper_summary: Concept-based Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) interprets deep learning models using human-understandable visual features (e.g., textures or object parts) by linking internal representations to class predictions, thereby bridging the gap between low-level image data and high-level semantics. A major challenge, however, is the reliance on large sets of labeled images to represent each concept, which limits scalability. In this work, we investigate the use of zero-shot Text-to-Image (T2I) generative models as a source of synthetic concept datasets for concept-based XAI methods. Specifically, we generate concepts using predefined prompts and evaluate their faithfulness to real ones through four complementary analyses: (1) comparing synthetic vs. real concept images via concept representation similarity; (2) evaluating their intra-similarity by comparing pairs of subsets of the same concept with progressively increasing size; (3) evaluating their performance for downstream explanation tasks using relevant class images; (4) evaluating how removing a concept from tested class images affects explanations of generated concepts. While current T2I generative models promise a shortcut to concept-based XAI, our study highlights challenges and raises open questions about the use of synthetic data generated by zero-shot pipelines in model analyses. The resulting dataset is available at https://github.com/DataSciencePolimi/ZeroShot-T2I-Concepts.
2605.19855.paper_summary_zh: 概念基礎的可解釋人工智慧（XAI）透過將內部表示與類別預測聯繫起來，使用人類可理解的視覺特徵（例如，紋理或物體部分）來解釋深度學習模型，從而彌合低階影像數據與高階語義之間的差距。然而，一個主要挑戰是依賴大量標記圖像來代表每個概念，這限制了可擴展性。在本研究中，我們調查了使用零樣本文本到影像（T2I）生成模型作為概念基礎的 XAI 方法的合成概念數據集來源。具體而言，我們使用預定義的提示生成概念，並通過四種互補分析評估其對真實概念的忠實度：（1）通過概念表示相似性比較合成與真實概念圖像；（2）通過比較同一概念的子集對，評估其內部相似性，並逐步增加大小；（3）使用相關類別圖像評估其在下游解釋任務中的表現；（4）評估從測試類別圖像中移除概念如何影響生成概念的解釋。雖然當前的 T2I 生成模型為概念基礎的 XAI 提供了一條捷徑，但我們的研究突顯了挑戰並提出了關於在模型分析中使用零樣本管道生成的合成數據的未解決問題。所得到的數據集可在 https://github.com/DataSciencePolimi/ZeroShot-T2I-Concepts 獲得。
2605.19855.author: Giacomo Astolfi et.al.
2605.19855.authors: Giacomo Astolfi, Matteo Bianchi, Riccardo Campi, Antonio De Santis, Marco Brambilla
